behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves source meaning, not just literal word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural translation model output quality (grammar + semantic fidelity). A mock or deterministic stub cannot expose model judgment failures or output variability.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation API on a bilingual evaluation set with idioms and non-literal phrases; score semantic preservation and fluency against acceptance thresholds, and fail if outputs are literal-but-wrong or ungrammatical.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns HTTP 503, the service transparently falls back to dictionary translation and does not error to caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes HTTP status handling and transport/client behavior. In-process mocks can miss incorrect 503 mapping, retry/exception translation, or fallback trigger conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service with a real HTTP client pointed at a test server that returns 503; seed SQLite fallback entries; call translate endpoint and assert successful response with fallback translation (no propagated error).

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: This depends on SQL schema/query wiring and DB I/O. Mocking repository calls can hide real query/key/encoding mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temporary SQLite DB with production schema, insert known phrase->translation rows, invoke fallback translator module, and assert exact returned translation for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Computation plus persistence must both work together. Unit-only checks on distance math can miss serialization/table/transaction bugs when storing metrics.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality-tracking path with fixed primary/fallback strings and a real local SQLite metrics table; assert persisted edit-distance value matches expected Levenshtein result.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module orchestration and async background execution (response prioritization + deferred metric logging). No model-judgment dependency is required for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run service components with deterministic primary and fallback adapters plus real async executor and SQLite; assert API response returns primary translation immediately, then assert quality metric appears asynchronously afterward.
